summary: Cathy O'Neil interviews her husband Johan de Jong about the Stacks Project, an open-source algebraic geometry textbook that has evolved from a simple PDF download site into an interactive platform with sophisticated visualizations. The project is notable for its neurotic rigor—every result has a proof and references are strictly linear. Pieter Belmans has added features including full-text search, tag lookup, commenting, and most impressively, dynamic dependency graphs using d3.js. The new visualizations show how mathematical results depend on each other through heat maps colored by distance from root or leaf nodes, collapsible chapter-based views, and rotating graphs labeled by result type.
key_points: The Stacks Project is a self-contained, strictly linear exposition of algebraic geometry where all references point to earlier results
Every mathematical result has a permanent four-letter tag that serves as a stable reference even as content is added
New d3.js visualizations show logical dependencies between results through multiple heat map styles based on distance metrics
The graphs allow users to explore how sophisticated results depend on foundational lemmas across different chapters
The project evolved from simple file downloads to an interactive platform with search, commenting, and dynamic graph generation
money_quotes: We were quite neurotic setting it up – everything has a proof, other results are referenced explicitly, and it's strictly linear.
Of course, any mathematician thinks we can do that for all of mathematics.
I even had this deal where, if people found mathematical mistakes in the Stacks Project, they'd get a free t-shirt, and I'm happy to report that I just last week gave away my last t-shirt.
The redder results are the results that are closer to it in meaning and sophistication level.
For all of the graphs, hovering over a result will cause the statement of the result to appear, which is awesome.
key_insight: Mathematical knowledge can be structured as a strictly linear, fully-proven dependency graph, and visualizing these logical relationships reveals meaningful patterns about how sophisticated results build on foundational concepts.
